hero.title: Access Michigan Services, Simplified
hero.subtitle: One structured gateway to health, housing, food, and family services across all 83 counties.

access.sectionTitle: Where do you want to start?
access.sectionSubtitle: Three pathways to the services Michigan residents need most.
access.housingDesc: Emergency shelter, rental assistance, utility programs, and pathways to stable housing across Michigan.

access.healthDesc: Find doctors, clinics, mental health support, prenatal care, and insurance enrollment options nearby.

systems.title: What Is Access Michigan?
systems.description: Access Michigan is an independent, non-commercial resource that organizes public service information from across state agencies into a single, structured interface. It does not replace government services — it makes them easier to find, understand, and act on.

prototype.title: Public Beta:
prototype.description: Built by a Michigan resident, improving every week with community feedback and open data integration.

quickActions.transportationDesc: Public transit, school bus safety, automated enforcement, accessible rides for seniors

quickActions.civicDataDesc: State budget, FOIA requests, voter info, elected officials, public meetings
quickActions.financialHelpDesc: Free and reduced-cost care, insurance enrollment, prescription assistance
quickActions.communityResourcesDesc: Food, housing, transportation, support groups, social services

trust.dataFrom: Data from CMS, HRSA, CDC, Michigan DHHS
trust.privacyFirst: No Cookies · No Personal Data Collection

footer.disclaimer: This platform is an independent, non-commercial civic resource. It does not collect personal data, use cookies for tracking, display advertising, or sell information.
footer.noAds: No advertising · No data selling · Built to serve Michigan residents
footer.ethicalNote: This project is built with an ethical, non-commercial ethos — prioritizing public benefit over profit.
footer.dataSourceNote: Some metrics may reflect a 3–12 month lag from source agencies.
footer.brandDescription: An independent civic resource organizing Michigan's public services into one structured, accessible interface.
footer.privacyStatement: No cookies, no tracking, no personal data collected.
footer.dataAttribution: Data: CMS · HRSA · CDC · MDHHS · Leapfrog · EPA · NHTSA · EIA

healthMap.title: Health Map
healthMap.description: Explore healthcare facilities across Michigan. Toggle layers to show different facility types. Markers are color-coded by quality score.
healthMap.dataNoticeText: Facility names and locations are real Michigan healthcare facilities sourced from CMS Hospital Compare. Quality scores reflect available public data from CMS, Leapfrog Group, ANCC Magnet Recognition, and BCBSM Blue Distinction. Some metrics may have a 3–12 month lag from source agencies.
healthMap.demoScores: Quality data sourced from CMS, Leapfrog, and ANCC.

environment.improvingTrendDesc: Violations decreased 50% since 2019 with ongoing Flint, Benton Harbor, and statewide infrastructure investments.

aboutPage.title: Helping Michigan Families Navigate Health, Safety & Transportation
aboutPage.subtitle: An independent, data-driven civic resource built to improve health, safety, and transportation access for all Michigan residents — regardless of income, insurance status, or background.
aboutPage.missionDesc: Access Michigan exists to help Michigan residents navigate healthcare, energy, transportation, safety, and public services — finding quality resources, accessing financial assistance, and making informed decisions. We believe everyone deserves access to trustworthy information, regardless of income, insurance status, or background.
aboutPage.independentDesc: No advertising, no paid placements. Rankings based on publicly available, third-party quality data.
aboutPage.residentFirstDesc: Every design decision optimizes for the person seeking care — not for health systems or insurers.
aboutPage.dataDrivenDesc: All data sources cited. Methodology published. Rankings explainable and reproducible.
aboutPage.rankingDesc: When you search for care, our platform ranks results using a weighted composite score based on four objective dimensions. The highest-quality, most accessible, most comprehensive providers naturally rank first — not because they paid us, but because they objectively perform better on metrics that matter to patients.
aboutPage.userControlDesc: You can always re-sort by distance only, insurance accepted, quality score, or other dimensions. Safety-net options (FQHCs, community health centers, public hospitals) are never filtered out by default and receive dedicated callouts like "No one turned away" and "Sliding scale fees available."
aboutPage.dataSourcesSubtitle: All data publicly available and independently verifiable
aboutPage.portfolioDesc: Access Michigan is a working public beta — a community-oriented civic resource built to help Michigan residents navigate health, safety, energy, transportation, and public services. It is continuously improving with community feedback and open data integration.

methodologyPage.title: How We Transform Public Data Into Community Impact
methodologyPage.subtitle: A replicable framework for integrating fragmented datasets into accessible, equity-centered civic infrastructure.
methodologyPage.frameworkSubtitle: From raw public data to actionable community resource
methodologyPage.equitySubtitle: Every decision filtered through health equity

researchPage.title: Human-Centered Design for Health Equity
researchPage.subtitle: Research-driven insights centering the most vulnerable populations.
researchPage.personasSubtitle: Representative profiles from Michigan's most underserved populations
researchPage.journeysSubtitle: Emotional arcs of real user scenarios
researchPage.testingNote: Testing conducted with 12 participants: 4 uninsured individuals, 4 seniors, 4 community health workers. Remote moderated sessions using think-aloud protocol.

impactPage.title: Community Impact Through Data-Driven Design
impactPage.subtitle: Methodology-focused case studies demonstrating how integrated public data informs strategic planning and reduces health disparities. Methods replicable for health systems, government agencies, and community organizations.
impactPage.ambulatorySubtitle: Data-driven efficiency gains for ambulatory center management
impactPage.ambulatoryNote: Metrics derived from operations research modeling using public HPSA, transit, and utilization data. Projections based on peer-reviewed ambulatory optimization literature.

technicalPage.title: Built for Scale, Designed for Impact
technicalPage.subtitle: Production-grade architecture with public sector considerations.
technicalPage.archSubtitle: Four-layer stack with security at every tier
technicalPage.interopSubtitle: FHIR, HL7, and USCDI alignment for health system integration
technicalPage.interopNote: Architecture designed for future EHR/HIE integration. Data models map to FHIR R4 resources, enabling seamless interoperability with Trinity Health Epic, Corewell Health, and HFHS systems.

wizard.title: Find Programs for Me
wizard.intro: Answer 3 quick questions — we'll find programs matched to your situation. No sign-up. No data stored.
wizard.step1Title: What do you need help with?
wizard.step3Note: Used to show local programs. Not saved externally.
wizard.qualifyText: Based on your info, you may qualify for {{count}} programs:
wizard.emptyBody: We didn't find an exact match, but help is still available. Call 2-1-1 for a live referral.
wizard.disclaimer: These are estimates only. Final eligibility is determined by each program.
